invariants.description: Every custom ESLint rule must be registered in eslint.config.mjs
Single source of truth for ESLint-rule <-> Python-invariant pairing lives in invariants.json under _js_rules.
invariants.context: Single source of truth for ESLint-rule <-> Python-invariant pairing lives in invariants.json under _js_rules. Every file in src/scripts/eslint-rules/*.js (minus index.js) must appear there with a known status; every status='ported' entry must name a python_invariant id that exists; no _js_rules entry may point to a deleted rule file. Guards against drift between the two sides -- adding a rule on either side without updating the concordance fails the battery.

invariants.description: Must have at least 23 custom ESLint rules
HME Python files must not use silent `except Exception: pass` catch-all (swallows all errors). Matches both same-line and next-line forms, project-wide under tools/HME/service/server.
invariants.context: HME Python files must not use silent `except Exception: pass` catch-all (swallows all errors). Matches both same-line and next-line forms, project-wide under tools/HME/service/server. Promoted to error after the 149-hit backlog was cleared  any new instance is a regression.

invariants.description: HME Python files must not silently return an empty default (`[]`, `None`, `False`, `{}`, `''`, `dict()`, etc.) from an `except Exception` without calling `logger.*` or `print` first.
invariants.born_from: R33 silent fallback incidents in /home/jah/Polychron/i/review auto-fire, list_knowledge, and get_knowledge_status
invariants.context: HME Python files must not silently return an empty default (`[]`, `None`, `False`, `{}`, `''`, `dict()`, etc.) from an `except Exception` without calling `logger.*` or `print` first. R33 found 3 instances in the wild (i/review auto-fire, list_knowledge, get_knowledge_status); each degraded a substrate capability invisibly because the fallback value LOOKED legitimate. Sibling of hme-py-no-silent-catchall but distinct: that catches `pass`, this catches silent-return-default.

invariants.description: Project-wide ASCII enforcement. Strict: no allowlist, no emoji exceptions, no math-symbol exceptions. Source must be pure ASCII (0x20-0x7e + tab + LF + CR).
invariants.context: Project-wide ASCII enforcement. Strict: no allowlist, no emoji exceptions, no math-symbol exceptions. Source must be pure ASCII (0x20-0x7e + tab + LF + CR). Use the generalized tools/HME/scripts/audit-no-non-ascii.py which scans .py / .js / .sh / .json / .md / .txt under src + scripts + tools/HME. Mirrors src/scripts/eslint-rules/no-non-ascii.js for the JS half.

Python equivalent of ESLint local/no-doubled-fallback (src/scripts/eslint-rules/no-doubled-fallback.js). Detects `dict.get(key, DEFAULT) or X` where DEFAULT is nontrivial -- either the outer `or` is dead code OR it silently rewrites legitimate falsy values (empty list, 0, False) to X. The JS rule caught a regimeClassifier bug where every real 0-tension reading got coerced to 0.5; same class of bug in Python.

_js_rules._comment: Concordance map between custom ESLint rules (src/scripts/eslint-rules/*.js) and their Python invariants. Status values: 'ported' = has a Python equivalent registered under python_invariant; 'js_only' = architecturally JS-specific (composition engine, CommonJS, JS globals) or covered by Python language/tooling conventions (PEP8, raise semantics). This block is the source of truth for the README parity table and is validated by the eslint_concordance_complete invariant.

rules.name: no-empty-catch
rules.reason: Silent `except Exception: pass` is the direct Python analog of an empty catch block.

rules.name: no-silent-early-return
rules.reason: Silent `except Exception: return <empty default>` without a logger call is the Python form of the R33-class silent-failure pattern.

rules.name: no-non-ascii
rules.reason: Same copy-paste failure mode (NBSP, curly quotes, zero-width spaces) in Python source.

rules.name: no-doubled-fallback
rules.reason: `dict.get(k, DEFAULT) or X` silently coerces legitimate falsy values -- same class of bug as the regimeClassifier 0->0.5 tension incident that motivated the JS rule.

rules.name: no-or-fallback-on-config-read
rules.reason: Partial coverage via hme-py-no-os-environ (forces ENV.get() helper) and hme-py-no-doubled-fallback (catches the .get(k, D) or X variant). Python has no equivalent to the JS `_cc`/`_atsc` config-section naming convention, so a strict port would be noisy on legitimate `.get(k) or {}` idioms.

rules.name: only-error-throws
rules.reason: Python's `raise` statement already requires a BaseException subclass at runtime; raising a literal raises TypeError immediately. No static check needed.

rules.name: case-conventions
rules.reason: Python has PEP8 (snake_case for functions/vars, PascalCase for classes) enforced by ruff/flake8/black. Not re-enforcing in the battery.

rules.name: no-console-acceptable-warning
rules.reason: Python HME code uses structured logger.* calls, not console-equivalent `print`. The discipline is enforced by reviewing new `print(` calls, not by lint.

rules.name: no-math-random
rules.reason: No Math global in Python; deterministic-random discipline lives in pipeline determinism invariants instead.

rules.name: no-bare-math
rules.reason: No Math global in Python.

rules.name: no-typeof-validated-global
rules.reason: Boot-validated globals are a JS/CommonJS concept; Python has no `typeof` operator and no equivalent boot-validation chain.

rules.name: no-requires-outside-index
rules.reason: CommonJS require() load-order discipline is JS-specific; Python imports have no equivalent hazard.

rules.name: no-bare-l0-channel
rules.reason: L0 channels are a composition-engine concept living entirely in src/ (JS). No Python-side surface.

rules.name: no-direct-signal-read
rules.reason: conductorIntelligence.getSignalSnapshot is a JS composition-engine surface; no Python consumer.

rules.name: no-direct-buffer-push-from-crosslayer
rules.reason: crossLayerEmissionGateway is a JS-side boundary; Python pipeline scripts don't participate.

rules.name: no-direct-conductor-state-from-crosslayer
rules.reason: Conductor/cross-layer boundary lives entirely in src/.

rules.name: no-direct-crosslayer-write-from-conductor
rules.reason: Conductor/cross-layer boundary lives entirely in src/.

rules.name: no-direct-coupling-matrix-read
rules.reason: Coupling matrix firewall is a JS-only architectural rule covering src/ only.

rules.name: no-conductor-registration-from-crosslayer
rules.reason: Conductor registration surface is JS-only.

rules.name: no-unregistered-feedback-loop
rules.reason: feedbackRegistry is a JS-side registry; Python pipeline scripts declare loops via metrics/feedback_graph.json directly.

rules.name: no-unstamped-validator
rules.reason: The validator stamp chain is a JS-side invariant; Python has no equivalent validator factory.

rules.name: prefer-validator
rules.reason: validator.create pattern is JS-only.

rules.name: validator-name-matches-filename
rules.reason: JS-only naming discipline tied to validator.create('ModuleName').

rules.name: no-useless-expose-dependencies-comments
rules.reason: `// expose dependencies` comments are a JS/CommonJS boot-chain artifact; no Python analog.

rules.name: no-bare-declared-global-in-init
rules.reason: moduleLifecycle.declare init-closure deps discipline is a JS/CommonJS pattern; no Python analog.

rules.name: no-or-fallback-on-map-get
rules.reason: JS `Map.get(...) || literal` collapsing-zero pattern; Python `dict.get` defaults are explicit, no equivalent hazard.
